title: The Philosophy of Time
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Our (0) perception of time is often subjective. In moments of intense (17), an hour can feel like a mere minute. This (18) of time is a central theme in many philosophical (19). || Historically, the (20) of the clock brought a new (21) to daily life. No longer governed by the sun, human (22) became synchronized with mechanical gears. Despite this, the (23) of our internal biological rhythms cannot be (24). Nature still dictates our need for rest.

questions.explanation: Noun describing the state of being stiff/strict.
Plural noun for things people do.
Passive verb with prefix meaning 'ignored' or 'missed'.
